commit_scope: fourth-kernel.form M2 proof-of-shape: a grammar-as-data compiler (bmf-mini.fk) folds source tokens through rule cells into a program-as-cells; the pipeline compile -> fkc-emit-many -> clang yields a running binary, and swapping the grammar yields a different binary (the compiler-compiler property, in compiled output). Transform proven three-way at 15; binary generation driven end-to-end in the audit.

commands: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/minimal-surface.fk form-stdlib/fourth-walker.fk form-stdlib/bmf-mini.fk form-stdlib/tests/bmf-mini-band.fk
local_validation.summary: bmf-mini-band holds three-way at 15: (1) faithful — compile grammar g1 over source [1,1,2] and walk on 5 equals 22 equals the independently computed bmf-ref; (2) grammar is data — g2 (operand 100 not 10) over the SAME source walks to 202, the compiler-compiler property; (4) program-as-cell — same grammar+source twice is the same cell, a different grammar a different cell; (8) a mixed ADD/SUB fold walks to its reference. The audit drove the binary half end to end: grammar + source -> compile (recipe) -> fkc-emit-many -> clang -> running binary; g1 -> binary -> 22, g2 -> binary -> 202 on the same source [1,1,2] at input 5 — same source, different grammar, different working binary.

observed_delta.compiler_compiler_property: same source, different grammar -> different binary
observed_delta.north_star_ratchet: bands runnable on the fourth arm: 0 of 424 (M2 is the pipeline, not yet a band on fkw)

known_limitations.limitation: The compiler runs as a Form recipe (it folds Form lists of rules and tokens); the standalone compiler-binary that reads textual source through the port needs m4e2 (heap for the lists) + m4e4 (strings for textual source/output).
known_limitations.follow_up: When m4e2/m4e4 land, the compiler emits as a binary via the same fke machinery the walker uses — it is already program-shaped.

known_limitations.limitation: Tokenwise pattern-action fold — no nesting, choose, fail, or cut yet; this is the data-driven transform, not the full BMF engine.
known_limitations.follow_up: m4e3 reads engine.fk's apply-object-rule recipes through the observe door for the full nesting/backtracking BMF.

known_limitations.limitation: The grammar's op set is the walker's (ADD/SUB over a literal operand); richer semantic actions arrive as the walker op set grows (m4e2/m4e4).
known_limitations.follow_up: Each new op the walker gains is a new semantic-action shape the grammar can target.

ci_validation.summary: PR gates run the bmf-mini band three-way alongside the other fourth-kernel bands.

deploy_validation.summary: form-stdlib additions ride the api image rebuild; the lattice ingests the updated fourth-kernel.form on the post-merge hook.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: No route behavior changes. The body gains the compiler-compiler kernel as a proof-of-shape: grammar + source compiles to a running native binary, and the grammar genuinely drives the output (swap it, the binary changes) — M2's central claim, demonstrated.
test_flows: bmf-mini-band proves faithful transform vs reference, grammar-is-data, program-as-cell identity, and a mixed fold, three-way at 15.
fourth_kernel_audit.sh M2 section: two grammars over one source -> two binaries via clang -> 22 and 202, parity-gated.
e2e_validation.summary: validate.sh three-way 1 ok 0 divergent; audit end-to-end grammar+source -> binary, grammar-driven.

phase_gate.gate: grammar + source -> a running binary, grammar-driven, transform three-way; CI + deploy confirm the crossing
phase_gate.next: m4e2 heap so the compiler itself emits as a binary; m4e3 the full engine.fk BMF through the observe door
